title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Automation an… · Program Brief: Transport Abstraction
summary: A maintainable Python package needs a typed transport protocol, immutable configuration, operation methods, decorators, and explicit exception taxonomy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py transport abstraction increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers small composable client library boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

content.code:
# Worked focus: http-py-transport-abstraction — Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
def worked_http_py_client_architecture_4(base_url: str, timeout_ms: int, credential_provider_ready: bool) -> dict[str, object]:
    assert isinstance(base_url, str) and isinstance(timeout_ms, int)
    https = base_url.startswith("https://")
    valid = https and timeout_ms > 0 and credential_provider_ready
    origin = base_url.split("/", 3)[:3]
    return {"valid": valid, "origin": "/".join(origin) if len(origin) == 3 else "invalid"}
content.caption: Compile-ready deterministic HTTP decision model; trace each return, call it with one accepted and one rejected boundary, then state which live-network claim still needs transfer evidence.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A useful test seam requires a mock interface for every HTTP type. This remains false for Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers. This fails the Transport Abstraction contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
